prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms: O, C, H, H) were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fitted PySCF RKS backend), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N+1 (anion, charge −1, mult 2) and N−1 (cation, charge +1, mult 2) states were also computed for the finite-difference Fukui construction. Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme. The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz (single point, not re-optimized at this level). All three SCF runs converged; integrity check: trustworthy = true; sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000.

Atom-by-atom Fukui indices (Mulliken, B3LYP/def2-TZVP, gas phase):
  Atom 1 (O):  f+ = 0.46813824,  f− = 0.46742464,  f0 = 0.46778144,  dual = +0.00071360
  Atom 2 (C):  f+ = 0.70839130,  f− = 0.11244422,  f0 = 0.41041776,  dual = +0.59594709
  Atom 3 (H):  f+ = −0.088269392, f− = 0.21007563,  f0 = 0.060903121, dual = −0.29834503
  Atom 4 (H):  f+ = −0.088260145, f− = 0.21005551,  f0 = 0.060897683, dual = −0.29831566

Most electrophilic site: C (atom 2), f+ = 0.7083913 — the carbonyl carbon is the dominant site for nucleophilic attack, consistent with classical aldehyde reactivity.
Most nucleophilic site: O (atom 1), f− = 0.46742464 — the carbonyl oxygen is the primary site for electrophilic attack.
